title: PWmodernizer Plan Envelope
description: Machine-readable sidecar to the human-readable expected-plan.md. Implements the LPW (arXiv 2411.14503) / Routine (arXiv 2507.14447) inter-stage contract: a structured plan that Stage 2 (code generation) can be verified against. The markdown plan remains the reviewer-facing UX layer; this envelope is the validatable contract.

$schema.description: Optional JSON-Schema meta-reference. Stage-1 LLMs conventionally add this to associate the instance with its schema for editor tooling; with additionalProperties:false it would otherwise be rejected, blocking an otherwise-valid plan. Permitted but never required.

inputBasename.description: Basename of the source input. For single-file inputs this is the filename (e.g. 'input.spec.ts'). For multi-file inputs (directory inputs) this is the directory basename (e.g. 'input'). Stage 2 uses this to anchor the plan to a specific source unit.

sourceFramework.description: Framework the source input is written in. 'bad-playwright' is a subtractive migration (no framework translation, only smell removal). The other three require a framework translation.

subtractive.description: True iff sourceFramework === 'bad-playwright'. Validator enforces this invariant. Subtractive migrations cannot legally introduce new framework imports; non-subtractive migrations must.

scenarios.description: Test scenarios the generated spec must cover. Every id MUST appear as exactly one `// plan:scenario=<id>` comment in the generated test file.

id.description: Stable scenario identifier in N.M form (e.g. '1.1', '1.2'). Referenced by `// plan:scenario=<id>` comments in generated code.

title.description: Human-readable scenario title. Should match the eventual test('...') name in the generated spec.

userAction.description: One-sentence summary of what the test does from the user's perspective.

expectedAssertions.description: Observable outcomes the generated test MUST check. Corresponds to the User-perceivable assertion checklist in the markdown plan.

requiredPOMs.description: Page Object Model file paths Stage 2 must produce, relative to repo root. Accepts BOTH legacy v0.1.x path (`outputs/tests/pages/foo.page.ts`) AND pwm-blueprint v0.2.0 path (`outputs/helper/page-object/pages/foo.page.ts`). The validator's pwm-blueprint fallback maps the legacy path to the new location when checking on-disk existence (PR #156). Empty array when no POM extraction is justified.

requiredFixtures.description: Legacy fixture file paths (`outputs/tests/fixtures/...`) — preserved for v0.1.x compatibility. v0.2.0 pwm-blueprint target prefers `outputs/helper/fixtures/...` (e.g. base.fixture.ts).

requiredPages.description: pwm-blueprint Page Object Model files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/page-object/pages/dialog.page.ts'). Each extends BasePage with the no-constructor / readonly-fields / .describe() / [LABEL]-expects discipline from migration-rules.md §3.

requiredBlocks.description: pwm-blueprint Block files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/page-object/blocks/header.block.ts'). Each extends BaseBlock. Extract when a section reaches ~5+ locators or 3+ methods, or appears on 3+ pages.

requiredApi.description: pwm-blueprint API wrapper files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/api/accounts.api.ts'). One file per endpoint. Used for data preparation — the only sanctioned path; never UI-prep.

requiredActions.description: Cross-page flow files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/actions/add-product-to-cart.ts'). Extract when the journey crosses 2+ Pages.

requiredUtilities.description: Pure utility files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/utilities/parse-prices.ts'). Verb-prefixed names: parse*, get*, calculate*, verify*, generate*. Each requires a matching unit test file (100% coverage gate).

requiredTestData.description: Test-data files Stage 2 must emit or mutate (e.g. 'outputs/helper/test-data/labels.ts'). Constants only — URLs, ids, cookies, LABEL_* describe-prefixes, TestRail suite IDs.

requiredTypes.description: Type-shape files Stage 2 must emit (e.g. 'outputs/helper/types/external/accounts-api.ts' or 'outputs/helper/types/internal/parsed-price.ts').

locatorTable.description: Locator translation table: original locator strings mapped to their modern Playwright replacements with reviewer-readable confidence.

confidence.description: Reviewer's confidence that the translation is correct without DOM inspection. 'low' MUST be backed by a hallucination-defense pin.

notes.description: Free-form reviewer notes — DOM assumptions, fallbacks, etc.

hallucinationDefensePins.description: Explicit hallucination guards. Each pin documents an assumption Stage 2 is making about the runtime DOM, with a reviewer-fallback if the assumption is wrong.

pinId.description: Sequential pin number, unique within the envelope.

elementDescription.description: Human-readable description of the pinned element (e.g. 'Dashboard greeting').

assumedLocator.description: Locator Stage 2 will emit assuming the DOM matches expectations.

sourceLocator.description: Original locator from the source (preserved as fallback).

whyComment.description: WHY-comment Stage 2 must emit inline near the assumed locator, explaining the DOM assumption.

reviewerFallback.description: Concrete action the reviewer should take if the assumption fails (e.g. 'ask FE team to add data-testid="dashboard-greeting"').

expectedMetrics.description: Quantitative targets Stage 2's output is expected to hit. Used by scripts/evaluate.ts to score the migration.

selectorQualityScore.description: Fraction of locators using role/label/testid (vs CSS class / nth). 0.0-1.0.

smellCountDelta.description: Net change in smell count. Must be <= 0 (migrations never introduce smells).

locDelta.description: Net change in lines of code (output - input). Negative for typical subtractive migrations, positive when POM extraction adds files.
